text: Early sports specialization in youth athletes can lead to detrimental physical and psychological effects.
Sports specialization can be beneficial when the timing is appropriate and conditions are correct, allowing athletes to reach specific goals.
Intensive training in young athletes, whether sport-specialized or multi-sport, carries risk of adverse physical and psychological effects including overuse injuries, overtraining, and burnout.
Young athletes should be monitored for signs of overuse injuries, overtraining, and burnout regardless of whether they specialize in a single sport or participate in multiple sports.
